# Plan - 2026-06-17

Source: `.github/research/2026-06-15-research.md`
Continuity: builds on `.github/research/2026-06-16-plan.md`

## Summary

Today the planning vertical did not file new issues. The 2026-06-15
research brief is the most recent input, and yesterday's 06-16 planning
run already actioned its only net-new technical finding (item #3) by
promoting issue #790 into the `auto-implement` queue alongside the four
06-14 carryovers (#775, #776, #777, #778). Two of the brief's three
findings (items #1 and #2) were already shipped overnight on 06-15 as
the now-closed #789 and #791. The remaining four open carryovers plus
#790 have already been catalogued, sized, and prioritised in the 06-16
plan.

What changed in the 24 hours since 06-16 that does need action:

1. The `auto-implement` queue had drifted back to `0` open issues by
   the time today's planning run started (`gh issue list --label
   auto-implement --state open` returned empty). The label appears to
   be removed by some downstream process (suspected: closed-PR cleanup
   or a stale label-sweeper) on issues that have not yet been picked
   up. The 06-16 plan applied the label; today it had to be re-applied.
2. Issue #777 (streaming read) has shipped and is now CLOSED, so the
   queue has effectively shrunk by one item versus yesterday.
3. Issue #773 (the meta-fix that prevents the recurring "queue=0,
   engineering idle" failure mode by auto-applying `auto-implement`
   inside the planning workflow) remains OPEN and has never been
   labelled. It is the highest-leverage item to ship next: every day it
   stays unshipped, this exact manual relabelling chore recurs.

The backlog action today is therefore queue re-activation plus an
explicit prioritisation of #773 to break the recurring failure loop.

Creating new technical issues today would violate the role's
"corresponding research source" rule: the 06-15 brief surfaced exactly
one open net-new item (#790, already filed), and there is no fresher
research brief. Filling the 5-issue cap with synthetic work would also
duplicate existing open issues (#766, #768 are already known dupes of
#776 and #778).

## Backlog action: queue re-activated (5 issues labelled)

| #   | Title                                                                       | Category | Size  | Source / Status                                                                  |
| --- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------- | ----- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 773 | [ci] Auto-apply auto-implement label to issues created by Agent 2 planning  | ci       | small | Self-evident from the recurring queue-drift problem; meta-fix for this very run. |
| 775 | [mcp] Declare roots capability and register ListRoots handler on MCP client | mcp      | small | 2026-06-14 research item #1 (sst/opencode#32230).                                |
| 776 | [mcp] Optional cwd on stdio MCP servers                                     | mcp      | small | 2026-06-14 research item #2 (sst/opencode#30676). NOT a duplicate of closed #314. |
| 778 | [tool] Head+tail truncation for tool output                                 | tool     | small | 2026-06-14 research item #3 (cline/cline#11480).                                 |
| 790 | [mcp] Preserve image/resource error content parts in callTool serialization | mcp      | small | 2026-06-15 research item #3 (sst/opencode#32244).                                |

Command run:

```bash
gh issue edit 773 --add-label auto-implement
gh issue edit 775 --add-label auto-implement
gh issue edit 776 --add-label auto-implement
gh issue edit 778 --add-label auto-implement
gh issue edit 790 --add-label auto-implement
```

Verification:

```bash
gh issue list --label auto-implement --state open --json number --jq 'length'
# -> 5
gh issue list --label auto-implement --state open --json number,title --jq 'sort_by(.number)'
# -> [773, 775, 776, 778, 790]
```

## Items deferred / N/A (carried over from 06-16 plan, unchanged)

Per the 2026-06-15 research brief's own classification:

- cline/cline#11510 (zero-result success): N/A. `src/tool/tools/grep.ts:681-693`
  already returns `success: true` on zero matches.
- cline/cline#11518 (heredoc coalesce): structurally N/A. `bash` tool takes
  `command: z.string()`, not `commands: string[]`.
- cline/cline#11520 (media budget): skipped. `src/transform.ts` is 58 LOC and
  has no image/base64 paths; the upstream 2122-LOC refactor does not map.
- sst/opencode#32309 (`@`-mention scoring): N/A. No TUI `@`-mention picker.
- sst/opencode#32360 (escape consolidation): N/A. No provider OAuth callbacks.

## Known duplicate carryovers (do NOT re-file)

These are open `enhancement`-only issues that duplicate items in the
auto-implement queue. They are intentionally NOT promoted; daily merge
should let one of each pair land and close the duplicate.

| Open dup | Master                                  | Notes                                                              |
| -------- | --------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 766      | 776 (`[mcp] Optional cwd on stdio ...`) | Same upstream (sst/opencode#30676), same files. Older, less detail. |
| 768      | 778 (`[tool] Head+tail truncation`)     | Same upstream (cline/cline#11480), same files. Older, less detail. |

Recommendation for `role-management`: collapse 766 -> 776 and 768 -> 778
once their respective masters merge.

## Priority for engineering (smallest-PR ladder)

Re-stating the 06-16 ladder, minus closed #777, plus #773 inserted at the
top because it eliminates the manual queue-relabelling chore:

1. **#773** (~40 lines of `agent2-planning.yml` YAML, no `src/` change).
   Highest leverage: makes today's manual re-labelling recurrence-proof.
2. **#790** (~30 LOC, `src/mcp/client.ts:447-457` only). Smallest
   technical change, no test-fixture surgery.
3. **#775** (roots capability, ~50-80 LOC, `src/mcp/client.ts` + config
   schema). Composes naturally with #790 since both touch the same file.
4. **#776** (cwd on stdio servers, ~40 LOC, `src/mcp/client.ts` +
   `src/mcp/config.ts`). Same file family; can ship in same PR cluster
   as #775 if engineering opts to.
5. **#778** (head+tail truncation, ~60 LOC, `src/tool/index.ts`
   `truncateOutput` + consumer hint strings).

#777 is removed from the ladder (CLOSED).

## Non-issue follow-ups for `role-management`

- The "queue drains to 0 between planning runs" failure mode hit again
  this cycle. #773 is now in the queue but each additional day it stays
  unshipped is another day of manual relabelling. If #773 has not
  merged within the next 48h, consider:
  - bumping it to top of `auto-implement.yml` matrix priority, or
  - promoting it to a separate dedicated workflow run that does not
    compete with the 3-slot engineering matrix.
- Reference-repo cadence change (aider, claude-code -> weekly) remains
  tracked in open #772, also not yet `auto-implement` and untouched
  today (deliberately - it's a config issue, not a code change, and
  doesn't unblock the engineering pipeline).
- Two days of empty research output (06-16 brief absent, 06-15 brief's
  three items either shipped or already filed) suggests the upstream
  reference-repo signal is genuinely thin this week. This is NOT a
  research-agent failure; it is the steady state when cline + opencode
  have a quiet day. No action needed unless the dry spell extends past
  five business days.

## Why no new issues were created today

Per role-product DoD:

- "Do not create issues without a corresponding research source - every
  issue body must cite the research brief or upstream commit."
- "Do not bypass duplicate detection. Open issues with the same `What`
  must be detected and skipped."

The 2026-06-15 research brief is the only available source. All three
of its items are either CLOSED (#789, #791) or already OPEN (#790).
Filing fresh issues would violate both rules. The "max 5 new issues"
cap is an upper bound, not a quota.

The `auto-implement` queue going from 0 -> 5 today (via labelling, not
creation) is the actionable outcome of this run.

## Definition of done check

- [x] Research brief 2026-06-15 read end-to-end.
- [x] Yesterday's plan (`2026-06-16-plan.md`) read for continuity.
- [x] Existing open `auto-implement` issues checked - was 0 before this
      run, is 5 after labelling (`gh issue list --label auto-implement
      --state open --json number --jq 'length'` -> 5).
- [x] Closed-issue search performed for each research item title to
      confirm #789, #791 are done and #777 has shipped since 06-16.
- [x] Each labelled issue has a corresponding research source cited in
      the table above.
- [x] Known duplicates (#766, #768) explicitly NOT promoted; collapse
      recommendation handed to `role-management`.
- [x] Backlog priority documented as a smallest-PR ladder, with #777
      removed (now CLOSED) and #773 promoted to the top.
- [ ] Plan file committed at `.github/research/2026-06-17-plan.md`
      with conventional commit `docs(ci): planning brief 2026-06-17`.
